benchmark_report.title: 🔬 SAVAGE COMMAND BENCHMARKER - Scientific Roasting Report
benchmark_report.methodology: Surgical precision measurement with PhD-level statistical brutality
benchmark_report.disclaimer: All assessments backed by hard data. Feelings were not considered.

methodology_details.complexity_scoring: Based on CLAUDE.md rules: <5 points required
methodology_details.quality_metrics: Maintainability index, ROI, success rate predictions

individual_command_analysis.savage_analysis: This command shows some engineering discipline but still manages to lose money. ROI of -36.84% means you're literally paying to make things worse. The 15 thinking blocks suggest someone actually tried to think, which is refreshing but apparently insufficient.
evidence: Complexity score 3/5 - within acceptable range (barely)
65% success rate - better than a coin flip
Bloat factor 16.7 - acceptable verbosity
Has proper structure (context + task)
individual_command_analysis.statistical_significance: Within 1 standard deviation of mean quality
individual_command_analysis.recommendation: Acceptable for production but monitor ROI closely

individual_command_analysis.savage_analysis: This is not intelligence; this is a 665-line monument to over-engineering. With 29 MCP calls and a 10% success rate, you'd have better odds playing Russian roulette. The -88.93% ROI means you're basically setting money on fire while waiting 25+ seconds for inevitable failure.
evidence: Complexity score 7/5 - VIOLATES CLAUDE.md rules by 40%
Bloat factor 221.7 - more fluff than a pillow factory
29 MCP dependencies - this isn't AI, it's dependency hell
10% success rate - worse than random chance
25.68s execution time - users will have made coffee and forgotten what they asked
individual_command_analysis.recommendation: DELETE IMMEDIATELY. Rewrite from scratch with adult supervision.

individual_command_analysis.savage_analysis: 438 lines with ZERO thinking blocks. This isn't a command, it's a wall of text pretending to be software. -100% ROI is mathematically impressive - you've achieved perfect value destruction. No context, no task definition, just pure chaos.
evidence: 0 thinking blocks in 438 lines - thinking is apparently optional
Missing basic structure (no <context> or <task>)
-100% ROI - perfect value destruction
Bloat factor 438 - one continuous stream of consciousness
individual_command_analysis.recommendation: Burn it down. Start over with basic software engineering principles.

individual_command_analysis.savage_analysis: 27 thinking blocks suggest someone was actually trying to think, but a 20% success rate proves thinking and succeeding are different skills. Complexity score 6 violates CLAUDE.md rules, and -40.3% ROI means even 'senior' developers can lose money efficiently.
evidence: Complexity score 6/5 - violates CLAUDE.md by 20%
27 thinking blocks - at least someone was trying
20% success rate - 4 out of 5 attempts fail
Has proper structure (context + task + examples)
individual_command_analysis.recommendation: Reduce complexity or accept 80% failure rate. Your choice.

individual_command_analysis.savage_analysis: 357 lines with 1 thinking block. The irony is palpable - a command called 'ultrathink' that barely thinks. 73% success rate with -58.2% ROI means it occasionally works but always costs more than it delivers. Classic software engineering.
evidence: 1 thinking block in 357 lines - thinking is apparently rationed
Complexity score 6/5 - violates CLAUDE.md by 20%
Bloat factor 357 - the thinking-to-fluff ratio is embarrassing
-58.2% ROI - consistently losing money
individual_command_analysis.recommendation: Rename to 'ultrafluff.md' for accuracy. Then delete it.

roi_analysis.statistical_conclusion: All commands lose money with 95% confidence

best_command.name: java-rapid-implementation.md
best_command.caveat: Still loses money, just less aggressively

worst_command.name: ultrathink-full-mcp.md
worst_command.why: 665 lines of dependency hell with 10% success rate
worst_command.impact: Would cost organization $X per execution with 90% failure rate

most_ironic.name: ultrathink.md
most_ironic.why: Claims to think deeply, contains 1 thinking block in 357 lines

immediate_actions: Reduce complexity in 3 commands violating CLAUDE.md rules
Implement ROI monitoring for all commands
systemic_fixes: Enforce CLAUDE.md complexity limits in CI/CD
Implement automated bloat detection (>50 lines per thinking block)
Add ROI tracking to command metrics
quality_gates: ROI must be >0% for production deployment

final_verdict.primary_issue: Systematic over-engineering with negative economic impact
final_verdict.prognosis: Requires immediate intervention to prevent total system failure
final_verdict.savage_summary: You've successfully created a collection of commands that consistently lose money while violating basic engineering principles. The fact that some occasionally work is more luck than design. Congratulations on achieving negative value with statistical significance.
final_verdict.risk_if_ignored: Continued economic loss and user frustration
